workout.title: Professional training plan - personalized for you

workoutForm.title: Plan Form | Musclepals
workoutForm.description: Answer the questions to tailor the plan to your goals.

WorkoutPage.description: Complete a short quiz, and I'll create the perfect training plan for you! It'll take you 2-3 minutes.

bulk.label: I want to bulk
bulk.alt: I want to gain muscle mass
bulk.description: You focus on building new muscle mass, weight goes up.

cut.label: I want to cut
cut.description: You lose weight, shed fat, and reveal existing muscle.

Bodyfat.title:
Select your
body fat level
Bodyfat.alt: Physique for selected body fat level

stepsDataMale.info: Extremely low body fat. The physique is very muscular, and definition is excellent.
Very low body fat. The physique is lean, muscles are clearly visible.
Healthy, athletic body fat level. Muscles are visible, but the body isn't emaciated.
Low body fat level. Physique is slim, muscles are visible.
Moderate body fat level. Physique is fuller, muscles are less visible.
Higher body fat level. Physique is rounded, muscles are not visible.
High body fat level. The physique is very rounded.
Very high body fat level. The physique is obese.

stepsDataFemale.info: Very low body fat content, typical for physique athletes. May be associated with hormonal imbalances and amenorrhea.
Low body fat content, often seen in active women and athletes. Very slim physique with clearly defined muscles.
Healthy, athletic body fat level. Slim but feminine physique. Most common among active women.
Average body fat level. Natural physique, slightly rounded, without clear muscle definition.
Elevated body fat level. Clearly rounded physique, possible health issues related to excess fat.
Very high body fat level. Health consultation and lifestyle changes are recommended.

Weight.error2: Enter your target weight in the range of 30-300kg.
Weight.errorBulk: If your goal is to bulk, target weight must be higher than current weight.
Weight.errorCut: If your goal is to cut, target weight must be lower than current weight.

12-14.description: This is the best time to build solid foundations! Your body is still developing, so the key is habits and learning technique, not heavy weights.

15-20.message: Ideal age to get serious about training!
15-20.description: Your body recovers quickly, so you can make great progress with well-planned exercises and nutrition.

21-30.message: This is the time to build truly great form!
21-30.description: You already have body awareness - now you just need a well-tailored plan to take your results to the next level.

Activity.title: What is your physical activity level?
Activity.errorMsg: Please select one of the options before proceeding.

options.low_activity: I sit most of the day, little movement. 🛋️
options.some_activity: I move a bit, but no regular workouts. 🚶
options.light_training: I train lightly a few times a week. 🏃‍♂️
options.regular_training: I exercise regularly, I'm active most days. 💪
options.hard_work: I train very hard or have a demanding physical job. 🏋️‍♂️

description.starvation: The body is severely weakened due to extreme weight deficiency.
description.emaciation: The body is clearly weakened; muscles and tissues lack sufficient nutrition.
description.underweight: Body weight is too low. The organism isn't properly protected against diseases and handles physical exertion poorly.
description.normal: The body is neither burdened by excess body fat nor weakened by its deficiency.
description.overweight: Excess body fat appears, beginning to burden the body.
description.obesityI: Excess body fat clearly burdens the body and increases the risk of diseases.
description.obesityII: The body is heavily burdened. The risk of serious chronic diseases increases, and physical fitness is limited.
description.obesityIII: Very high amount of body fat seriously threatens health and life.

gym.label_f: I will train at the gym

gym.description: Training at the gym with access to a wide range of equipment.

bandsPanel.question: Do you have access to resistance bands?

Experience.title: What is your training experience?

none.label: Haven't started training yet. 🐣
none.description1: In the beginning, the key is learning proper technique and building consistency.
none.description2:
But don't worry!
Your Plan will guide you through this step by step ;)

just_started.label: Just started. 🏁
just_started.description2: At this stage, your body responds quickly to training, so we'll focus on learning technique and simple, repeatable habits.

some_experience.label: I train a bit already. 💪
some_experience.description2: This is the moment your body gets used to training — so it's key to introduce a plan with structure and progression.

advanced.label: I've been training for a long time. 🥇
advanced.description1: Great job! You already know what regularity and consistency look like.
advanced.description2: At this stage, we'll focus on optimization: a precise plan, progression, and recovery.

Experience.errorMsg: Please select your training experience before proceeding.

Equipment.title: What equipment do you have at home?
Equipment.subtitle:
(you can select more
than one option)

none.label: No access to any training equipment. 🚫
none.description: We will base the plan on bodyweight exercises and simple household items.

bands.label: Resistance bands.
bands.description: Mini bands and power bands will allow us to add resistance and scale exercise difficulty.

dumbbells.label: Dumbbells.
dumbbells.description: We'll use single and double-hand variations to fully utilize the dumbbells' potential.

barbell.label: Barbell and weights.
barbell.description: We'll set up classic lifts (squats, deadlifts, presses) adjusting volume to your goal.

bench.label: Bench press bench.
bench.description: A bench allows adding lying and incline exercises to better target the stimulus.

Equipment.errorMsg: Select at least one equipment option to proceed.

options.diet: I don't really know how to stick to a diet. ❓
options.no_difficulty: I don't have any of the above difficulties. ✅

Sleep.title:
How much time
do you spend sleeping?
messages: It's hard to recover well with this amount of sleep... Remember to work on it :)
Good amount of sleep — recovery supports training and health.
Very good amount of sleep — optimal recovery.
Too much sleep might mean irregularity — monitor how you feel.

ResultPage.title:
Your Training Plan
is ready!
ResultPage.intro1:
Created based on Your 14 answers by an advanced training algorithm
designed specifically for physique building and fat reduction.
ResultPage.intro2:
You will receive an individual plan
based on data, created in less than 3 minutes thanks to
technology that analyzes over
4,000 training combinations.
ResultPage.subtitle:
Quick Summary
of Your results
ResultPage.conclusion1: And then selects a specific training plan that fits Your situation better than 99% of cookie-cutter plans on the internet.
ResultPage.conclusion2: It's a combination of personal trainer knowledge and system precision that makes no mistakes and doesn't guess.
ResultPage.purchaseTitle: What exactly will you get after purchase?
ResultPage.sampleTitle: And here are a few pages from Rafael's plan!

MissingSteps.title: Some answers were not saved
MissingSteps.description:
Unfortunately, the software didn't save answers to the questions: {steps}.

Click the button below to go through these questions again.

persistentError.title: Problem persists
persistentError.description:
Unfortunately, the problem with saving answers persists. It might be a technical error.

Contact us at: {email}

PushupsStep.title: How many pushups can you do in one go?

PullupsStep.title: How many pullups can you do in one go?

descriptions.none: Starting from zero. Haven't trained regularly before and want to build basic strength and technique.
descriptions.just_started: Already have contact with sports. Can do a set of pushups and squats, but can't pull up at all or only 1-3 times.
descriptions.some_experience: Training regularly. Can comfortably do pullups (over 5) and dips. Want to level up (e.g. Muscle Up, L-sit).
descriptions.advanced: Basics are just a warmup. Can do Muscle Up and work on difficult statics (Front Lever, Planche) or advanced dynamics.

DurationStep.info: Optimal training time is 45-60 minutes. Remember that quality of sets is more important than quantity.
